finding: On independent clinical pathogenicity labels, the supervised predictor beats the zero-shot LM by +13.4pp AUROC — consistent with the molecular-property regime boundary (learned/AlphaMissense wins).
caveat: Single protein (MLH1, n=128). AlphaMissense training data likely overlaps clinical/population labels (home-field advantage), so this is a zero-shot-LM vs supervised-predictor comparison, NOT a held-out independent test for AlphaMissense. ESM |rho|=0.275 here vs 0.314 in the standalone pilot differs only by the inline-vs-score_assay masked-marginal implementation.
